SEARCH
Package com.falkordb

Class AsyncFalkorDB

java.lang.Object
com.falkordb.AsyncFalkorDB
public final class AsyncFalkorDB extends Object
Factory for AsyncGraph, an asynchronous view over a synchronous Graph.

The client stays a blocking, Java-8 client; asynchrony is provided entirely by the caller-supplied Executor. On JDK 21+ pass Executors.newVirtualThreadPerTaskExecutor() to fan a blocking graph out over many virtual threads: 


 Driver driver = FalkorDB.driver();
 ExecutorService pool = Executors.newVirtualThreadPerTaskExecutor(); // JDK 21+
 AsyncGraph async = AsyncFalkorDB.wrap(driver.graph("social"), pool);
 CompletableFuture<ResultSet> f = async.query("MATCH (n) RETURN count(n)");
 // ... on shutdown: drain outstanding futures, then pool.shutdown() and driver.close()

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static AsyncGraph
    wrap(GraphContextGenerator graph, Executor executor)
    Wraps a synchronous graph as an AsyncGraph whose operations run on executor.

  • Method Details

    • wrap

      public static AsyncGraph wrap(GraphContextGenerator graph, Executor executor)
      Wraps a synchronous graph as an AsyncGraph whose operations run on executor.

      Use a pool-per-call driver.graph(id) (a GraphContextGenerator), which is safe for concurrent calls; do not wrap a connection-bound GraphContext. The returned facade owns neither graph nor executor: the caller drains outstanding futures, shuts the executor down, then closes the graph and driver.

      Parameters:
      graph - the synchronous graph to wrap (typically driver.graph(id))
      executor - the executor each operation runs on (e.g. a virtual-thread executor on JDK 21+)
      Returns:
      an asynchronous view over graph
